summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that a JWT forged with the hardcoded secret 'frangoteam751' from server/api/jwt-helper.js:6 is accepted by a FUXA server running with security enabled. The forged admin token grants full access to admin-only endpoints, including user credential retrieval.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should cause the forged admin JWT (signed with hardcoded secret 'frangoteam751') to be accepted by the FUXA server, granting access to the admin-only GET /api/users endpoint and returning the full user list including password hashes.
actual_observed_result: With FUXA running (secureEnabled=true), a request to GET /api/users without authentication correctly returned HTTP 401. A second request with a JWT signed using the hardcoded secret 'frangoteam751' and admin claims {id:'admin', groups:[-1]} returned HTTP 200 with the admin user record including the bcrypt password hash. Server logs confirmed both the 401 (unauthenticated) and 200 (forged token accepted) responses.
verification_method: Two-step verification: (1) confirm that without a token the admin endpoint returns 401, proving security is active; (2) confirm that the same endpoint returns 200 with user data when presented with a JWT signed using the hardcoded secret, proving the secret enables token forgery. The distinct HTTP status codes (401 vs 200) distinguish a real exploit from a misconfiguration or startup failure.
evidence_produced_by_run: GET /api/users without token returned HTTP 401 with body {"error":"unauthorized_error","message":"Unauthorized!"}, confirming security is enabled.
GET /api/users with forged JWT (signed with 'frangoteam751') returned HTTP 200 with admin user data: [{"username":"admin","fullname":"Administrator Account","password":"$2a$10$...","groups":-1}].
FUXA server logs show: GET /api/users 401 (no token) followed by GET /api/users 200 (forged token accepted).
The proof string EVOHUNT_PROOF:hardcoded_jwt_secret was emitted upon successful exploitation.
limitations_or_blockers: The reproduction was verified locally using the FUXA server started from the target repository source with npm install. The Docker-based reproduction bundle (./reproduction/run.sh) follows the same flow but was not executed via Docker in this session.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
